segments.text: I THOROUGHLY ENJOY ASHLEY MORROW'S BLACK HISTORY PRESENTATIONS. BUT I ALWAYS FEEL BAD BECAUSE SHE COMES AND DOES ALL THAT TIME, I THINK IT IS IMPORTANT ENOUGH TO GIVE A LITTLE MORE WEIGHT. SHE'S ALWAYS GOOD ABOUT THE THREE-MINUTE THING. I WOULD LIKE TO ADD HER AS A RECURRING ITEM UNDER OUR PRESENTATIONS, COMMENDATIONS, PRESENTATIONS, FOR A BLACK HISTORY PRESENTATION FROM ASHLEY MORROW FOR THREE MINUTES SO SHE'S NOT SITTING HERE ALL DAY TO DO THAT. SHE COMES EVERY WEEK.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I THINK THAT'S FINE. MY ONLY CONCERN ABOUT THAT IS WE ONLY HAVE THREE SPOTS FOR COMMENDATIONS AND PRESENTATIONS.

segments.text: THEN THIS WOULD BE WAIVE THE RULES --

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: TO ADD THE FOURTH.

segments.text: ONLY IN SESSION YOU'RE ALLOWED TO DO THAT. IT IS YOUR RULES.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: YOU'RE LOOKING TO CHANGE THE RULE. YOU WANT TO CHANGE THE RULE.

segments.speaker: ALAN CLENDENIN
segments.text: LET'S NOT CALL IT COMMENDATION PRESENTATION. LET'S JUST SAY AFTER COMMENDATIONS AND PRESENTATIONS, WE'LL ADD A SPOT AFTER COMMENDATIONS AND PRESENTATIONS TO ALLOW ASHLEY MORROW TO PRESENT THREE MINUTES FOR BLACK HISTORY.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: COULD WE DISCUSS THIS OFF-LINE AND BRING IT BACK AT THE NEXT REGULAR MEETING? GOOD IDEA BUT FIGURE OUT HOW BEST TO DO THAT.

segments.text: WHY ISN'T IT A GOOD IDEA?

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: BECAUSE WE'RE GOING TO BE DOING AN UPDATE -- OR YOU'RE GOING TO BE ASKED TO UPDATE THE RULES.

segments.speaker: ALAN CLENDENIN
segments.text: I GET IT.

segments.speaker: BILL CARLSON
segments.text: WHAT ABOUT DOING IT STARTING NEXT THURSDAY BECAUSE IT IS BLACK HISTORY MONTH.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: YOU COULD PUT IT ON FOR PRESENTATION AND PUT HER UP -- FULL OF PRESENTATIONS FOR NEXT WEEK?

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: NO, WE HAVE WORKSHOPS.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: THERE ARE. MY EYES POPPED OUT. THE 26th, BY THE WAY, COUNCIL, YOUR RULES STATE THAT YOU HAVE TO HAVE AS MANY ITEMS AS YOU WANT TO GET OUT BY 1:00. YOU HAVE NINE ITEMS.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I JUST MOVED ONE.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: THAT'S EIGHT ITEMS BY 1:00.

segments.text: COUNCIL MEMBER CARLSON'S IS A WRITTEN REPORT. SO THAT'S TWO DOWN.

segments.text: SEVEN ITEMS BY 1:00.

segments.speaker: LYNN HURTAK
segments.text: I'M SAYING, THOUGH, WE CUT TWO DOWN. WOULD YOU BE WILLING TO HOLD ON TO THAT?

segments.speaker: ALAN CLENDENIN
segments.text: I RESCIND THE MOTION AND HOLD ON UNTIL MR. SHELBY AND I HAVE AN OPPORTUNITY TO SPEAK ABOUT IT. REFERENCE THESE AGENDAS AND ALL THESE THINGS. WE'RE GOING TO FIGURE OUT A WAY TO KEEP THESE THINGS FROM HAPPENING AGAIN BECAUSE THIS IS CRAZY, UNLESS YOU WANT TO GO TO MIDNIGHT. WE HAVE A MOTION TO RECEIVE AND FILE. MR. SHELBY.

segments.speaker: MARTIN SHELBY
segments.text: CAN'T HELP BUT ADD THAT IF THIS WERE A NIGHT MEETING NIGHT, YOU WOULD BE AN HOUR AND 50 MINUTES INTO YOUR NIGHT MEETING.

segments.text: THAT'S WHY WE DON'T DO NIGHT MEETINGS ON REGULAR MEETINGS.

segments.text: THE WAY THINGS ARE GOING, YOU MAY HAVE TO ADD TO THEM.

segments.speaker: ALAN CLENDENIN
segments.text: BEFORE WE GET TO RECEIVE AND FILE, I WANT TO ASK YOU GUYS ON STAFF REPORTS IF, ONE, DO YOU NEED THE INFORMATION? THINK ABOUT IT BECAUSE OF THIS WORKLOAD. TWO, IF YOU NEED THE INFORMATION, CAN IT BE WRITTEN? IF IT CAN BE WRITTEN, PLEASE, PLEASE, PLEASE MAKE IT A WRITTEN REPORT BECAUSE THE STAFF REPORTS ARE TAKING WAY TOO MUCH TIME. I WANT TO REMIND EVERYBODY THAT WHEN WE ALLOW FOR COMMENDATION, WE LIMIT THE COMMENDATIONS TO HOW MANY MINUTES, SULING? FIVE MINUTES. SO WE HAVE TO REALLY GET THAT DOWN. THAT'S PART OF WHAT ATE UP OUR TIME THIS MORNING. WE'LL WORK ON SOME OF THE OTHER THINGS AS WE MOVE FORWARD. MR. SHELBY, THOUGHTS?

segments.text: JUST ALSO THERE ARE COUNCIL'S OWN RULES AND THEY ARE RELATIVELY RECENT, BUT I HAVEN'T LOOKED AT THEM TOO LONG, IF IT IS A NON-CITY BUSINESS COMMENDATION IT REQUIRES A MEMO IN ADVANCE FROM THE COUNCIL MEMBER TO THE COUNCIL AS TO WHO IT IS AND WHY THEY NEED IT.
